title: craspedophyllum cheesemanii
intro: Craspedophyllum cheesemanii, also known as Cheeseman's craspedophyllum, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Craspedophyllum cheesemanii include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Traditional herbal preparations of Craspedophyllum cheesemanii include teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water.
